question: In your own words, describe the fundamental concept of garbage collection in Java. What problem does it solve for a programmer?
expectedPoints: Relieves programmer from manual deallocation duty

question: Explain how an object becomes eligible for garbage collection. Describe at least three common scenarios that make an object unreachable.
expectedPoints: An object is eligible when no live thread can access it (unreachable)
An object created inside a method (local variable) after method completion

question: Compare and contrast the finalize() method and the System.gc() method. Explain the role of each in the garbage collection process, their intended usage, and why both are generally discouraged in modern Java programming.
expectedPoints: finalize(): cleanup method called by GC before object destruction; unreliable and deprecated
System.gc(): hint to JVM to run GC; not a guarantee
finalize() can resurrect objects and cause performance issues
System.gc() is often ignored by the JVM and disrupts efficient GC algorithms
Modern best practice: avoid both; use try-with-resources and explicit close() methods for cleanup
